provenance.author_model: unknown (commits carry no model trace; Hunter pipeline typically Codex)

lenses.name: double-count / dedupe key stability
lenses.reason: diff adds multi-source connectors sharing recordNewInvocations dedupe

lenses.name: registry id / flag consistency
lenses.reason: new ids vs AGENT_FLAGS and LEGACY_IDS

lenses.name: resolveRegistryDir token coverage
lenses.reason: vendored JSON tokens vs resolver prefixes

lenses.name: complexity budget / perf
lenses.reason: scan was 25s cold; new connectors must be cache-gated

lenses.name: error-path forcing (missing/corrupt sources)
lenses.reason: each count/scan claims 0 on absent source

lenses.name: new-domain matrix
lenses.reason: no validator widened; connectors add new domains covered by the double-count lens

lenses.name: force-red
lenses.reason: all new tests are birth tests for new parsers; no fix to revert against. The dedupe drift has no guarding test, reported as finding F1/F2

deterministic.reason: 6 errors 9 warnings; in-diff: registry.ts format+organizeImports, sync-agent-registry.ts 4x noNonNullAssertion + noAssignInExpressions; rest preexisting (gemini/cursor/claude/auto-scan/index AlreadyTracked already orphaned in main)
3 NEW errors in new files: amp.ts:51, copilot.ts:45, openhands.ts:37 (string|undefined vs string|null, same class as exempted codex.ts:41 but new code)
both exit 0; cold --full scan 24.5s (matches 25s baseline), cached 0.1s
182 changed, 28 impacted, 844 edges, unresolvedCalls 2321 >> edges: map under-covers (dynamic import of registry.ts is invisible); top items runScan/performScan/scanInstalledSkills all inspected

findings.claim: Cline-family: one skill invocation recorded twice per task (ui_messages.json real ts + api_conversation_history.json fallback ts produce distinct trackedSet keys); deduplicateInvocations cannot clean it (groups by exact timestamp)
findings.evidence: repro: scanClineFamilyTasks over the repo's own cline-task fixture recorded resend x2 and deslop x2 for one task

findings.claim: Windsurf ItemTable chat bubbles re-record every historical invocation on every scan where state.vscdb fingerprint changed (fallbackTimestamp = new Date() per scan); unbounded growth for active Windsurf users, fired automatically by SessionEnd auto-scan
findings.evidence: repro: two parses of the unchanged windsurf-chatdata fixture 1.1s apart recorded resend twice

findings.claim: Continue sessions not in sessions.json index use file mtime as the single timestamp for all invocations; an active session re-records its full history each scan while it keeps changing
findings.evidence: repro: same session file, mtime moved, second scan recorded the same call1 invocation again

findings.claim: 4 of 9 new connectors (cline, kilocode, copilot, openhands) unreachable via agent filter: AGENT_FLAGS has no flags for them, while README claims 'Filter any command by agent' over the full table; --kiro and --antigravity flags map to ids with no connector so they silently scan nothing
findings.evidence: tui/args.ts AGENT_FLAGS enumerated vs registry.ts connector ids; args.ts absent from diff

findings.claim: Regression vs main: skillkit --kiro matched installed-skill detection in main (hardcoded id 'kiro'); registry-driven skills.ts now yields id 'kiro-cli' with no LEGACY_IDS mapping, so --kiro matches nothing
findings.evidence: main:skills.ts had { agent: 'Kiro', id: 'kiro' }; generated JSON only has kiro-cli; LEGACY_IDS lacks the entry

findings.claim: 3 new tsc errors in new connector files (string|undefined not assignable to string|null on regex match[1])

Loose SKILL.md-path text extraction (requireKnown:false) records invocations from mere path mentions in assistant prose, and the auto-scan garbage cleanup only covers codex/cursor/gemini, never the 9 new agents
findings.evidence: repro recorded x-momentum from a plain string mention and tiktok from a read path; auto-scan.ts:177 DELETE clause agent IN ('codex','cursor','gemini')

findings.claim: Kilo Code may double-count migrated sessions: scanKiloSessions sums legacy task dirs + kilo.db unconditionally, unlike goose which treats the db as single source of truth once it exists; countKiloSessions definitely sums both. Whether the same invocation lands twice depends on whether kilo's legacy migration preserves original timestamps and keeps legacy dirs on disk
findings.evidence: gap: kilo upstream migration semantics (packages/kilo-vscode/src/legacy-migration/task-store.ts) not fetched/verified in this run

exemptions.claim: Preexisting Biome errors (gemini/cursor/claude noAssignInExpressions+noNonNullAssertion, auto-scan noUnusedImports, index.ts AlreadyTracked) are not this diff's debt
exemptions.evidence: files untouched by diff; AlreadyTracked had exactly 1 occurrence (declaration only) in main too

exemptions.claim: queries.test.ts / burn.ts / codex.ts:41 tsc errors exempted per task brief
exemptions.evidence: verified all listed errors match the brief's exemption list

exemptions.claim: cursor and windsurf dedicated connectors do NOT collide with cline-family's Cursor/Windsurf globalStorage bases
exemptions.evidence: cursor reads ~/.cursor/projects jsonl; windsurf reads state.vscdb; cline-family reads globalStorage/saoudrizwan.claude-dev/tasks -- disjoint stores, cline tasks in Cursor storage are correctly attributed to cline

exemptions.claim: goose has no db+legacy double count
exemptions.evidence: scanGooseSessions returns after db path when sessions.db exists; legacy files only scanned when db absent (goose.ts:363-368)

exemptions.claim: resolveRegistryDir handles every token in the vendored JSON
exemptions.evidence: all 73 non-null globalSkillsDir values start with ~/, $XDG_CONFIG/, $CODEX_HOME/ or $CLAUDE_HOME/; sync script returns null for unresolvable expressions and skills.ts skips nulls

exemptions.claim: no perf regression: new connectors are all cache-gated per file or per db fingerprint
exemptions.evidence: cold --full 24.5s equals the 25s pre-diff baseline; cached 0.1s; every new scan loop calls cache.shouldSkip before parsing

exemptions.claim: crash safety holds for missing and corrupt sources
exemptions.evidence: full scan on machine with zero new-connector sources exits 0; corrupt sessions.db and missing db both return 0 via scanGooseDb

issue_candidates.title: roundTs-based dedupe key is structurally fragile for any connector whose two views of one event carry timestamps >1s apart
issue_candidates.evidence: trackedSet key skillName::second-rounded-ts; F1/F2/F3 are three instances of the same root cause; a per-event stable id (sessionId+callId) would fix the class
issue_candidates.why_out_of_scope: recordNewInvocations and roundTs predate this diff; the diff only multiplied the exposure

issue_candidates.title: copilot connector dedupes skill.invoked/tool-path per session (seenSkills) but the knownSkills branch bypasses push and records every occurrence
issue_candidates.evidence: copilot.ts:122-131 pushes directly to results, skipping the seenSkills guard used by every other branch
issue_candidates.why_out_of_scope: internal inconsistency with no confirmed miscount repro in this run; behavior question (dedupe-per-session vs count-each) for the author

issue_candidates.title: cline registry entry maps to ~/.agents/skills == sharedDir, so getDetectedAgents('cline') always returns [] even with Cline installed
issue_candidates.evidence: skills.ts:47 dir===sharedDir continue; cline's globalSkillsDir in the JSON is ~/.agents/skills
issue_candidates.why_out_of_scope: intended per the double-count comment, but the user-visible effect (cline never 'detected') is a design question, not a diff defect
